hints.to say: 'to say' is 'dicere' — do not confuse with 'facere'
hints.to become: 'to become' is 'fieri' — the passive of 'facere', but 'fecisti' is active

hints.Present indicative: Present would be 'facis' — 'fecisti' with the -isti ending is perfect
hints.Future perfect indicative: Future perfect would be 'feceris' — 'fecisti' is perfect indicative
hints.Pluperfect indicative: Pluperfect would be 'feceras' — 'fecisti' is perfect indicative

hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the parts of 'ferre' (to carry) — 'fecisti' comes from 'facere'
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become) — the passive of 'facere', but 'fecisti' is active
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the parts of 'fugere' (to flee) — a different verb entirely

hints.Nominative: 'nos' can be nominative, but here it is the direct object of 'fecisti'
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'nobis' — 'nos' is accusative here as the object of 'fecisti'
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'nobis' — 'nos' is accusative as the direct object

hints.Accusative: Accusative of a 3rd declension neuter looks the same, but 'cor' is the subject of 'est inquietum'
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'cordis' — 'cor' is nominative here
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'corde' — 'cor' is the nominative subject

hints.2nd: 2nd declension neuters end in -um — 'cor, cordis' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'cor, cordis' is 3rd declension neuter
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'cor' is 3rd declension

hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere/esse' — 'est' here is from 'esse' (to be)

hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect of 'esse' would be 'fuit' — 'est' is present indicative
hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'est' is indicative
hints.Imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'erat' — 'est' is present indicative

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — 'est' here is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go) — not related to 'est'
hints.possum, posse, potui: These are the parts of 'posse' (to be able) — a compound of 'esse' but different

hints.to ask: 'to ask' is 'rogare' or 'requirere' — 'requiescere' means to rest
hints.to require: 'to require' is 'requirere' — do not confuse with 'requiescere' (to rest)

hints.Present indicative: Present indicative would be 'requiescit' — the -at ending signals subjunctive
hints.Future indicative: Future would be 'requiescet' — 'requiescat' is present subjunctive
hints.Perfect subjunctive: Perfect subjunctive would be 'requieverit' — 'requiescat' is present subjunctive

hints.quaero, quaerere, quaesivi, quaesitum: These are the parts of 'quaerere' (to seek) — not related to 'requiescere'
hints.requiro, requirere, requisivi, requisitu: These are the parts of 'requirere' (to require) — do not confuse with 'requiescere'
hints.cresco, crescere, crevi, cretum: These are the parts of 'crescere' (to grow) — a different -scere verb

translation: You have made us for yourself, and our heart is restless until it rests in you.
